mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or if the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch that triggers inventory drawdowns and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if joint INDPRO declines and RSAFS surges consistently precede positive SPX 60-day returns in more than half of observed instances, or if joint Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of resilient aggregate demand and construction sector profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if HOUST increases fail to generate positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if the forward return spread between rising and falling HOUST periods is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows sequential growth, or if the rolling Rank IC over a 5-year window remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.0% threshold, or if the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while real GDP growth decelerates signals a productivity shock that eventually triggers wage-push inflation and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the joint PAYEMS acceleration and GDPC1 deceleration pattern precedes positive SPX 20-day returns in more than half of observed instances, or if the joint Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects rebuilding capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO increases fail to generate positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if the forward return spread between rising and falling INDPRO periods is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.10).
